name: Bruschetta with Braised Escarole & Roasted Garlic
recipeIngredient: 1.5 lb Escarole (trimmed and cut into 1-inch pieces)
4 French bread slices (4" x 6", halved and toasted)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Cut off about 1/2 inch from the top end of each garlic head, away from the root end. Remove any loose papery outer skin.
3. Place the cut sides of the garlic up in a small glass baking dish. Drizzle with 1 teaspoon olive oil. Cover with foil and roast for 30 minutes.
4. Turn the garlic cut side down and roast uncovered until tender, about 30 more minutes. Let cool to room temperature.
5. Separate the garlic cloves and place them in a bowl. Mash with a fork. Add 1 teaspoon olive oil, then season with salt and pepper (can be prepared up to 3 days ahead; cover and refrigerate, bringing to room temperature before using).
6. Cook escarole in a large pot of boiling salted water until just tender, about 4 minutes. Drain well.
7. Heat remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil in a heavy large skillet over medium heat. Add the minced garlic clove and sauté until heated through, about 3 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Spread roasted garlic puree on bread slices. Using tongs, place escarole atop the garlic. Serve with lemon wedges.
